work.title: Organization of work: from the weekly plan to the protocol
work.lead: Taska helps to build a rhythm: employees prepare plans, managers collect a picture by department, and the results of the gliders turn into clear protocols and tasks.
work.s1Body: Each division has a clear cycle: what we plan, what we do, what we deliver. The organizational structure in the system coincides with reality — it is easier to appoint responsible persons and not to lose instructions between "chats".
work.s2Body: Employees fix the priorities of the week in advance: key tasks, risks, dependence on colleagues. To the planner, the manager sees not oral reports, but structured input — saving time at the meeting and fewer surprises.
work.s3Body: At the department level, blockers, load balancing, and solutions to contentious topics are discussed. Everything that requires continuation is immediately formalized by tasks with deadlines and responsible ones — without "agreed verbally".
work.s4Body: The results can be collected in the protocol: decisions, deadlines, responsible persons. The document is tied to the department and period, it is seen by the team and management. Repeated topics from past weeks are not lost — it is easier to track performance.
work.s5Body: The same contour connects the funnel, finance, warehouse and processes: tasks are born from the protocol that fall into the work of performers and into the reports of the manager. This is how managed operating systems are built, not scattered tables.
